channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Proposed Tobacco-Free Generation Faces Industry Resistance
item.description: A growing movement advocates for creating a tobacco-free generation through policies aimed at preventing young people from ever starting to smoke. This approach, while not a guaranteed solution, has g...

item.title: Investigation into Tshwane water-tanker payments and potential family links
item.description: The City of Tshwane faces scrutiny over large payments made to private water-tanker companies. Eunice Nontobeko Mkhonza received R95 million for supplying water, while Mandla Jeffrey Mgcina, potential...

item.title: Adams Marine dey boost career and training opportunities for Nigerian seafarers
item.description: Adams Marine don become one major game changer for Nigeria maritime industry, as dem dey open doors for officers, cadets, and ratings to find better career and training chances. For many years, plenty...

item.title: ANC Greater Tshwane supports TMPD witness interviews at Madlanga Commission into corruption
item.description: The ANC Greater Tshwane Region has expressed support for the recent testimonies from the Tshwane Metro Police Department (TMPD) witnesses before the Madlanga Commission, which investigates corruption ...

item.title: Prime Minister Modi mourns the passing of veteran singer Asha Bhosle, recalling her vast musical legacy
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi paid heartfelt tribute to legendary Indian playback singer Asha Bhosle, who passed away recently. In a message shared on X (formerly Twitter), the Prime Minister expressed...

item.title: Exploring São Miguel Island: Hiking the Trails and Volcanoes of the Azores
item.description: Last November, a Canadian couple revisited their love for hiking on São Miguel Island in Portugal’s Azores, an archipelago known for its volcanic landscape and lush greenery. Free from family commitme...

item.title: SERAP calls on President Tinubu to investigate alleged diversion of ₦2.9 billion in NIGCOMSAT and NNRA funds
item.description: The Socio-Economic Rights and Accountability Project (SERAP) don dey call on President Bola Tinubu to order proper investigation into the alleged missing or diverted ₦2.9 billion public funds from Nig...

item.title: Political Leaders Condole the Demise of Legendary Singer Asha Bhosle
item.description: Legendary Indian singer Asha Bhosle, aged 92, passed away at Mumbai's Breach Candy Hospital on Sunday due to cardiac and respiratory issues, where she had been admitted in critical condition since Sat...

item.title: Justin Bieber Returns to Coachella and Performs With The Kid Laroi
item.description: American pop superstar Justin Bieber made a highly anticipated return to the main stage at Coachella 2026, marking his first full performance at the Californian festival since a cameo in 2022. Taking ...

item.title: US-Iran peace negotiations in Islamabad conclude without agreement after extended 21-hour session
item.description: Twenty-one hours of high-stakes peace negotiations between the United States and Iran have ended in Islamabad without a formal agreement. US Vice President JD Vance confirmed that Tehran had declined ...

item.title: Understanding Legal Requirements for Signalling While Driving in Canada
item.description: In Canada, the legal requirements for using vehicle turn signals vary by province. In Ontario, drivers must signal when turning, changing lanes, or pulling over, but only if their actions may affect o...

item.title: Kenya promotes overseas work for citizens while migrant protections lag
item.description: Labour migration from Kenya has shifted over decades, initially targeting Africa, North America, and Europe, but moving increasingly to Gulf countries like Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, UAE, and Oman since th...

item.title: Ouaddou acknowledges the challenge as Pirates fall short against Richards Bay
item.description: Orlando Pirates coach Abdeslam Ouaddou has conceded that clinching the Betway Premiership title is becoming more challenging following their 2-2 draw with Richards Bay. The Buccaneers needed a stoppag...

item.title: Nine unusual cosmetic surgeries people are actually doing in 2026
item.description: For real, the beauty and cosmetic surgery scene don change levels for 2026. Na not only the normal nose jobs or small tweaks dey happen again. People dey now pay to change body parts wey dem think fit...

item.title: Quebec Implements Ban on Religious Symbols for Daycare Workers
item.description: Quebec has recently passed legislation prohibiting daycare employees from wearing visible religious symbols such as hijabs, kippahs, or turbans. The legislation is part of the province's broader effor...

item.title: McIlroy Ties for Lead at Masters as Cameron Young Gains Momentum
item.description: Rory McIlroy experienced a challenging round at the Masters, causing him to falter slightly but still maintain a share of the lead. Meanwhile, Cameron Young displayed impressive form, rapidly climbing...

item.title: Government allows Form Four graduates to adjust subject combinations and college courses online
item.description: The Tanzanian government has opened a period for 2025 Form Four graduates to make changes to their Form Five subject combinations and courses in middle-level and technical colleges via the online Stud...
